title: azolla pinnata subsp. asiatica
intro: Azolla pinnata subsp. asiatica, also known as duckweed azolla, is a type of floating aquatic plant that belongs to the family Azollaceae. It has been traditionally used in Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of azolla pinnata subsp. asiatica include reducing blood sugar levels, improving lipid profiles, and exhibiting antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. In herbal preparations, azolla pinnata subsp. asiatica can be used in teas, infusions, or as a supplement, often in combination with other herbs, to leverage its medicinal benefits.
